agents.name: 513Sips Board
agents.purpose: Four-persona strategic review board — CEO, CFO, Hard Sell, Brand Director. Run before major decisions. Pulls in SME agents for deep-dives.

agents.name: Finance & Tax
agents.purpose: Tax strategy, complex accounting, multi-year financial planning. Consult for Board reviews and year-end prep.

agents.name: Pricing
agents.purpose: Quote generation, package building, margin analysis. Cross-reference Pricing-Strategy note for current rates.

agents.name: Contract
agents.purpose: Contract generation and management. Legal language for customer and vendor agreements.

agents.name: Operations Manager
agents.purpose: Day-to-day ops, scheduling, vendor coordination. Event logistics and supply chain.

agents.name: Marketing
agents.purpose: Brand voice and campaigns. Messaging strategy, positioning, campaign briefs.

agents.name: Quote
agents.purpose: Client quote generation. Builds packages from event specs using current pricing model.

agents.name: Event Recap
agents.purpose: Post-event documentation. Captures event details, notes, and outcomes for the record.

agents.name: Drink / Mixology
agents.purpose: Mixology brain: drink recommendations, quantities, batch prep, event drink logs.

agents.name: Event Post-Mortem
agents.purpose: 28-question guided debrief after every event. Captures what worked, what didn't, and what to improve.

agents.name: Customer Acquisition
agents.purpose: Lead channels, venue scouting, Google reviews management, outreach templates. Drives new business pipeline.

agents.name: Social Media
agents.purpose: Instagram content from Cloudinary photos + blog posts. Competitive analysis against PoppinBar and CincySips.

agents.name: Travel Planner PM
agents.purpose: Feature roadmap, sprint prioritization, and product decisions for the Travel Planner app.

agents.name: Travel Planner Dev
agents.purpose: Technical architecture and implementation for the Travel Planner app. React + TypeScript + Vercel stack.

agents.name: Engagement Manager
agents.purpose: Orchestrates the full pre-call workflow. Give it a prospect name + URL and it sequences brand research, site audit, and competitor analysis into a ready-to-use call package.

agents.name: Brand Intelligence
agents.purpose: Reviews a prospect's website, Facebook, and Instagram. Outputs tone, culture, platform, customer profile, and recent business activity.

agents.name: Site Audit
agents.purpose: Audits a prospect's site for broken links, SEO issues, missing CTAs, mobile problems, page speed, and outdated content. Outputs a scored report.

agents.name: Competitor Analysis
agents.purpose: Identifies 2–3 direct competitors, benchmarks their sites against the prospect's, and produces a gap summary showing where the prospect is losing ground.

agents.name: Standards
agents.purpose: Source of truth for deliverable templates, tone rules, scoring rubrics, pricing tiers, and prototype layout standards. Every other WebGrowth agent references this.

agents.name: Brain Librarian
agents.purpose: Save protocol enforcement, deduplication, vault maintenance. Keeps the Obsidian Brain clean and current.

agents.name: Session Summarizer
agents.purpose: Session handoff generation. Creates the closing summary saved to Brain at the end of every session.

agents.name: Social Media Manager
agents.purpose: Cross-project social media principles. Project-specific agents (513Sips) handle execution.

board.lastDecision: Implement accrual-basis accounting in Books. No double-counting confirmed. Owner reimbursement widget added ($326.92 due to Eddie).

tips.current: You invoke the Board after decisions are made — using it to validate work already done.
tips.better: Run it before major sessions. Pre-session alignment means you build the right thing first time instead of course-correcting mid-build.

tips.current: 513Sips and Brain have no CLAUDE.md — Claude rebuilds context from scratch at the start of every session.
tips.better: Add CLAUDE.md to both. 20 lines covering purpose, paths, and build commands saves 5+ minutes of ramp-up every session — it compounds fast.

tips.current: Handoffs capture what was done but not why decisions were made.
tips.better: Add a 'decisions + reasoning' field. In 3 months you'll know why you chose accrual-basis accounting — not just that you did.

tips.current: You're likely using one model for everything — builds, strategy, quick lookups.
tips.better: Sonnet for builds/saves/drafts. Opus for Board reviews and complex strategy. Haiku for quick lookups and data formatting. Match cost to task depth.

tips.current: You have 22 agents defined. Several have never been invoked in a real session.
tips.better: Archive agents unused in 30+ days. A smaller, sharper fleet is faster to navigate and forces clarity on when each agent actually gets used.

tips.current: Customer Outreach and Social pipelines are in the backlog but not built — intel is manual or missing.
tips.better: Build the outreach loop before the social pipeline. Weekly competitive intel from PoppinBar/CincySips feeds content strategy automatically.

tips.current: Sessions start with Claude asking clarifying questions before getting to work.
tips.better: Open with: 'Working on [X]. Last session: [Y]. Today: [Z]. Start with [first task].' Eliminates warm-up entirely — first pass is production-quality.

bestPractices.title: Opus 4.7 vs Sonnet 4.6
bestPractices.body: Opus: Board reviews, pricing strategy, legal review, complex architecture. Sonnet: builds, saves, blog work, routine tasks. Haiku: quick lookups, formatting, data transformation.

bestPractices.title: Agent Prompt Structure
bestPractices.body: Best agents have: (1) Role + persona, (2) Scope boundaries — what they do AND don't, (3) Output format, (4) 2–3 example invocations. Skip any and outputs get inconsistent.

bestPractices.title: CLAUDE.md Anatomy
bestPractices.body: Every CLAUDE.md needs: project purpose, local folder paths, active branch conventions, key files not to touch, and the build/deploy command. 20 focused lines beats 200 generic ones.

bestPractices.title: Session Handoff Value
bestPractices.body: Write handoffs as if a stranger is picking up tomorrow with no context. What do they absolutely need to know? Everything else is noise. The handoff is the session's entire lasting value.

bestPractices.title: Scheduled Agents
bestPractices.body: Scheduled agents are only valuable if you review their output. Build the review habit first (check the Automation Feed weekly), then scale up scheduled agents. Output without review is waste.
